``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined Sentencing judges have no discretion to set or influence the actual durations of the prison sentences they impose for each crime. “Judicial” maximum prison sentences must be fixed at the same level as the statutory maximum penalty for the offense of conviction. Any applicable minimum terms to parole-release eligibility are also preset by statute. Parole release dates are determined by the parole board.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Demographics ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 13%, changing from 6,807 in 2010 to 5,911 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series

# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases increased 34%, dropping from 3,108 in 2010 to 4,156 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and hispanic, any race people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Utah. The largest change was for other or unknown offenses, which increased by 3114%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Utah, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 8.7 times higher and Hispanic, any race people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 1.9 times higher and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 2.4 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 2.3 years. Black, non-Hispanic individuals experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 1.8 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For Murder and Non-negligent Manslaughter offenses, Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals had 8.21 more years on average compared to Hispanic, any race individuals, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
